text: {"matches":[{"type":"equivalent","fromIndex":19,"toIndex":20,"rationale":"Both state that 18.8% of students use AI daily."},{"type":"equivalent","fromIndex":16,"toIndex":47,"rationale":"Both report 95.6% of respondents use AI in academic activities."},{"type":"equivalent","fromIndex":17,"toIndex":48,"rationale":"Both report 88.2% of respondents use virtual assistants."},{"type":"equivalent","fromIndex":34,"toIndex":50,"rationale":"Both indicate 48.2% of students are concerned about the accuracy/incorrectness of AI‑generated content."},{"type":"possibly-equivalent","fromIndex":1,"toIndex":53,"rationale":"Both list significant AI benefits (personalized learning, engagement); 53 adds access to resources, a minor extension."},{"type":"narrower","fromIndex":55,"toIndex":2,"rationale":"Claim 55 mentions a subset of challenges (accuracy, over‑dependence, critical‑thinking erosion) that are included in the broader list of challenges in claim 2."},{"type":"narrower","fromIndex":45,"toIndex":2,"rationale":"Claim 45 focuses on the risk of over‑reliance hindering critical thinking, which is one of the broader challenges described in claim 2."},{"type":"broader","fromIndex":46,"toIndex":24,"rationale":"Claim 46 says a significant number view AI positively, while claim 24 gives a specific 80% agreement, making 46 more general."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":35,"toIndex":36,"rationale":"Both report 16.5% of students worried, but about different issues: critical‑thinking impact vs over‑dependence."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":24,"toIndex":27,"rationale":"Both give percentages of student agreement, but 24 refers to overall educational experience, whereas 27 refers to academic performance."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":24,"toIndex":31,"rationale":"Both provide percentages of agreement, yet 24 concerns educational experience while 31 concerns learning efficiency."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":34,"toIndex":44,"rationale":"Claim 34 gives a specific 48.2% concerned about incorrect answers; claim 44 expresses the general primary concern about data reliability."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":41,"toIndex":42,"rationale":"Both describe benefits of AI tools, but 41 emphasizes time reduction, whereas 42 emphasizes simplification of complex concepts."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":10,"toIndex":11,"rationale":"Both discuss AI‑driven personalized learning, yet claim 10 focuses on intelligent tutoring systems providing feedback, while claim 11 describes adaptive platforms tailoring content."},{"type":"broader","fromIndex":54,"toIndex":24,"rationale":"Claim 54 states most students view AI positively, whereas claim 24 provides the specific 80% figure, making 54 a broader summary."}]}
